data.body:
Root cause confirmed: cd "$MAIN_REPO" at line 938 is inside _phase_sync. On --resume when sync is already complete, _phase_sync is skipped entirely — the cd never executes. All subsequent phases (_phase_merge, _phase_version_bump, _phase_validate, _phase_push) inherit the worktree CWD. git push in the worktree dir targets the worktree branch (no upstream configured) instead of main — fails non-fast-forward.

Fix: added explicit cd "$MAIN_REPO" at the start of _phase_merge (after _state_write_phase). This makes CWD-correctness self-contained: _phase_merge always enters MAIN_REPO regardless of whether _phase_sync was skipped. All downstream phases inherit MAIN_REPO CWD from _phase_merge.

RED tests: tests/scripts/test-merge-to-main-resume-cwd.sh tests 1+2 (structural: _phase_merge has cd before git merge). GREEN after fix.
